abort.already_spooked: Yeah... well... no. K, thx, bye!

user.description: Did you read the readme? Did you read the license? Are you really sure?

choice_restart.description:
In order for Spook to finish setting up, Home Assistant needs to be restarted.

Do you want to restart Home Assistant now?

automation_unknown_area_references.description:
Spook has found a ghost in your automations 👻

While floating around, Spook crossed path with the following automation:

[{automation}]({edit}) (`{entity_id}`)

This automation references the following areas, which are unknown to Home Assistant:

{areas}



To fix this error, [edit the automation]({edit}) and remove the use of these non-existing areas.

Spook 👻 Your homie.
automation_unknown_area_references.title: Unknown areas used in: {automation}

group_unknown_members.description:
Spook has found a ghost in your groups 👻

While floating around, Spook crossed path with the following group:

{group} (`{entity_id}`)

This group has members, which are unknown to Home Assistant:

{entities}



To fix this error, edit the group, remove the use of these non-existing entities and restart Home Assistant.

Spook 👻 Your homie.
group_unknown_members.title: Unknown group members in: {group}

integration_unknown_source.description:
Spook has found a ghost in your Riemann sum integral helpers 👻

While floating around, Spook crossed path with the following helper:

{helper} (`{entity_id}`)

This helper has a source entity unknown to Home Assistant:

`{source}`



To fix this error, edit the helper and adjust the source entity (or remove the helper) and restart Home Assistant.

Spook 👻 Your homie.
integration_unknown_source.title: Unknown source: {helper}

proximity_unknown_ignored_zones.description:
Spook has found a ghost in your proximity configuration 👻

While floating around, Spook crossed path with the following proximity configuration:

{name}

This configuration is ignoring zones that are unknown to Home Assistant:

{zones}



To fix this error, edit the configuration and remove this/these zones.

Spook 👻 Your homie.
proximity_unknown_ignored_zones.title: Unknown ignored zones: {name}

proximity_unknown_tracked_entities.description:
Spook has found a ghost in your proximity configuration 👻

{entities}



To fix this error, edit the configuration and remove this/these entities.

Spook 👻 Your homie.
proximity_unknown_tracked_entities.title: Unknown tracked entities: {name}

proximity_unknown_zone.description:
Spook has found a ghost in your proximity configuration 👻

While floating around, Spook crossed path with the following proximity configuration:

{name}

This configuration is based on a zone that is unknown to Home Assistant:

`{zone}`



To fix this error, as you can't change the zone of a proximity configuration, the only option is to remove this specific proximity integration instance.

Spook 👻 Your homie.
proximity_unknown_zone.title: Unknown zone: {zone}

confirm_restart.title: Restart required
confirm_restart.description:
In order for Spook 👻 to finish setting up, Home Assistant needs to be restarted.

Select submit to restart Home Assistant now.

boo.name: Boo!
boo.description: Calling this action spooks Home Assistant. Performing this action will always fail.

random_fail.name: Random fail
random_fail.description: Performing this action will randomly fail.

url.name: URL
url.description: The URL to import the blueprint from.

homeassistant_create_area.name: Create an area
homeassistant_create_area.description: Creates a new area on the fly.

name.name: Name
name.description: The name of the area to create.

icon.name: Icon
icon.description: Icon to use for the area.

aliases.name: Aliases
aliases.description: A list of aliases for the area. This is useful if you want to use the area in a different language or different nickname.

homeassistant_add_alias_to_area.name: Add an alias to an area
homeassistant_add_alias_to_area.description: Adds an alias to an area.

area_id.name: Area ID
area_id.description: The ID of the area to add the alias to.

alias.name: Alias
alias.description: The alias (or list of aliases) to add to the area.

homeassistant_remove_alias_from_area.name: Remove an alias from an area
homeassistant_remove_alias_from_area.description: Removes an alias from an area.

area_id.name: Area ID
area_id.description: The ID of the area to remove the alias from.

alias.name: Alias
alias.description: The alias (or list of aliases) to remove from the area.

homeassistant_set_area_aliases.name: Sets aliases for an area
homeassistant_set_area_aliases.description: Sets aliases for an area. Overwrites and removes any existing aliases, fully replacing them with the new ones.

area_id.name: Area ID
area_id.description: The ID of the area to set the aliases for.

aliases.name: Aliases
aliases.description: The aliases to set for the area.

homeassistant_add_device_to_area.name: Add a device to an area
homeassistant_add_device_to_area.description: Adds a device to an area. Please note, if the device is already in an area, it will be removed from the previous area.

area_id.name: Area ID
area_id.description: The ID of the area to add the device to.

device_id.name: Device ID
device_id.description: The ID of the device(s) to add to the area.

homeassistant_remove_device_from_area.name: Remove a device from an area
homeassistant_remove_device_from_area.description: Removes a device from an area. As a device can only be in one area, this call doesn't need to specify the area.

device_id.name: Device ID
device_id.description: The ID of the device to remove the area from.

homeassistant_add_entity_to_area.name: Add an entity to an area
homeassistant_add_entity_to_area.description: Adds an entity to an area. Please note, if the entity is already in an area, it will be removed from the previous area. This will override the area the device, that provides this entity, is in.

area_id.name: Area ID
area_id.description: The ID of the area to add the entity to.

entity_id.name: Entity ID
entity_id.description: The ID of the entity (or entities) to add to the area.

homeassistant_remove_entity_from_area.name: Remove an entity from an area
homeassistant_remove_entity_from_area.description: Removes an entity from an area. As an entity can only be in one area, this call doesn't need to specify the area. Please note, the entity will still be in the area of the device that provides it after this call.

entity_id.name: Entity ID
entity_id.description: The ID of the entity (or entities) to remove the area from.

homeassistant_delete_area.name: Delete an area
homeassistant_delete_area.description: Deletes an area on the fly.

area_id.name: Area ID
area_id.description: The ID of the area to delete.

config_entry_id.name: Config entry
config_entry_id.description: The integration configuration entry to disable.

config_entry_id.name: Config entry
config_entry_id.description: The integration configuration entry to enable.

homeassistant_disable_device.name: Disable a device
homeassistant_disable_device.description: Disables a device on the fly.

homeassistant_enable_device.name: Enable a device
homeassistant_enable_device.description: Enables a device on the fly.

device_id.name: Device ID
device_id.description: The raw device ID to enable. Disabled devices are not shown by Home Assistant's device selector. Multiple IDs can be provided as a YAML list.

homeassistant_disable_user.name: Disable a user
homeassistant_disable_user.description: Disables a user account, preventing them from logging in.

user_id.name: User ID
user_id.description: The ID(s) of the user(s) to disable.

homeassistant_enable_user.name: Enable a user
homeassistant_enable_user.description: Enables a user account, allowing them to log in.

user_id.name: User ID
user_id.description: The ID(s) of the user(s) to enable.

homeassistant_update_entity_id.name: Update an entity's ID
homeassistant_update_entity_id.description: Updates an entity's ID on the fly.

new_entity_id.name: New Entity ID
new_entity_id.description: The new ID for the entity.

homeassistant_disable_entity.name: Disable an entity
homeassistant_disable_entity.description: Disables an entity (or entities) on the fly.

homeassistant_enable_entity.name: Enable an entity
homeassistant_enable_entity.description: Enables an entity (or entities) on the fly.

homeassistant_hide_entity.name: Hide an entity
homeassistant_hide_entity.description: Hides an entity (or entities) on the fly.

homeassistant_rename_entity.name: Rename an entity
homeassistant_rename_entity.description: Renames an entity (or entities) on the fly.

name.name: Name
name.description: The new name for the entity/entities.

homeassistant_unhide_entity.name: Unhide an entity
homeassistant_unhide_entity.description: Unhides an entity (or entities) on the fly.

homeassistant_create_floor.name: Create a floor
homeassistant_create_floor.description: Creates a new floor on the fly.

name.name: Name
name.description: The name of the floor to create.

icon.name: Icon
icon.description: Icon to use for the floor.

level.name: Level
level.description: The level the floor is on in your home.

aliases.name: Aliases
aliases.description: A list of aliases for the floor. This is useful if you want to use the floor in a different language or different nickname.

homeassistant_add_alias_to_floor.name: Add an alias to a floor
homeassistant_add_alias_to_floor.description: Adds an alias to a floor.

floor_id.name: Floor ID
floor_id.description: The ID of the floor to add the alias to.

alias.name: Alias
alias.description: The alias (or list of aliases) to add to the floor.

homeassistant_remove_alias_from_floor.name: Remove an alias from a floor
homeassistant_remove_alias_from_floor.description: Removes an alias from a floor.

floor_id.name: Floor ID
floor_id.description: The ID of the floor to remove the alias from.

alias.name: Alias
alias.description: The alias (or list of aliases) to remove from the floor.

homeassistant_set_floor_aliases.name: Sets aliases for a floor
homeassistant_set_floor_aliases.description: Sets aliases for a floor. Overwrites and removes any existing aliases, fully replacing them with the new ones.

floor_id.name: Floor ID
floor_id.description: The ID of the floor to set the aliases for.

aliases.name: Aliases
aliases.description: The aliases to set for the floor.

homeassistant_add_area_to_floor.name: Add an area to a floor
homeassistant_add_area_to_floor.description: Adds an area to a floor. Please note, if the area is already on a floor, it will be removed from the previous floor.

floor_id.name: Floor ID
floor_id.description: The ID of the floor to add the area on.

area_id.name: Area ID
area_id.description: The ID of the area(s) to add to the floor.

homeassistant_remove_area_from_floor.name: Remove an area from a floor
homeassistant_remove_area_from_floor.description: Removes an area from a floor. As an area can only be on one floor, this call doesn't need to specify the floor.

area_id.name: Area ID
area_id.description: The ID of the area to remove the floor from.

homeassistant_delete_floor.name: Delete a floor
homeassistant_delete_floor.description: Deletes a floor on the fly.

floor_id.name: Floor ID
floor_id.description: The ID of the floor to delete.

homeassistant_create_label.name: Create a label
homeassistant_create_label.description: Creates a new label on the fly.

name.name: Name
name.description: The name of the label to create.

icon.name: Icon
icon.description: Icon to use for the label.

color.name: Color
color.description: Color to use for the label. Can be a color name from the list, or a hex color code (like #FF0000).

homeassistant_add_label_to_area.name: Add a label to an area
homeassistant_add_label_to_area.description: Adds a label to an area. If multiple labels or multiple areas are provided, all combinations will be added.

label_id.name: Label ID
label_id.description: The ID(s) of the label(s) to add the area(s).

area_id.name: Area ID
area_id.description: The ID(s) of the area(s) to add the label(s) to.

homeassistant_add_label_to_device.name: Add a label to a device
homeassistant_add_label_to_device.description: Adds a label to a device. If multiple labels or multiple devices are provided, all combinations will be added.

label_id.name: Label ID
label_id.description: The ID(s) of the label(s) to add the device(s).

device_id.name: Device ID
device_id.description: The ID(s) of the device(s) to add the label(s) to.

homeassistant_add_label_to_entity.name: Add a label to an entity
homeassistant_add_label_to_entity.description: Adds a label to an entity. If multiple labels or multiple entities are provided, all combinations will be added.

label_id.name: Label ID
label_id.description: The ID(s) of the label(s) to add the entity/entities.

entity_id.name: Entity ID
entity_id.description: The ID(s) of the entity/entities to add the label(s) to.

homeassistant_remove_label_from_area.name: Remove a label from an area
homeassistant_remove_label_from_area.description: Removes a label from an area. If multiple labels or multiple areas are provided, all combinations will be removed.

label_id.name: Label ID
label_id.description: The ID(s) of the label(s) to remove from the area(s).

area_id.name: Area ID
area_id.description: The ID(s) of the area(s) to remove the label(s) from.

homeassistant_remove_label_from_device.name: Remove a label from a device
homeassistant_remove_label_from_device.description: Removes a label from a device. If multiple labels or multiple devices are provided, all combinations will be removed.

label_id.name: Label ID
label_id.description: The ID(s) of the label(s) to remove from the device(s).

device_id.name: Device ID
device_id.description: The ID(s) of the device(s) to remove the label(s) from.

homeassistant_remove_label_from_entity.name: Remove a label from an entity
homeassistant_remove_label_from_entity.description: Removes a label from an entity. If multiple labels or multiple entities are provided, all combinations will be removed.

label_id.name: Label ID
label_id.description: The ID(s) of the label(s) to remove from the entity/entities.

entity_id.name: Entity ID
entity_id.description: The ID(s) of the entity/entities to remove the label(s) from.

homeassistant_delete_label.name: Delete a label
homeassistant_delete_label.description: Deletes a label on the fly.

label_id.name: Label ID
label_id.description: The ID of the label to delete.

homeassistant_ignore_all_discovered.name: Ignore all currently discovered devices
homeassistant_ignore_all_discovered.description: Ignore all currently discovered devices that are shown on the integrations dashboard. This will not ignore devices that are discovered after this.

domain.name: Integration domain
domain.description: The integration domain to ignore all discovered devices for. If not provided, all domains will be considered to be ignored.

homeassistant_disable_polling.name: Disable polling for updates
homeassistant_disable_polling.description: Disables polling for updates for an integration configuration entry.

config_entry_id.name: Config entry
config_entry_id.description: The integration configuration entry to disable polling for.

homeassistant_enable_polling.name: Enable polling for updates
homeassistant_enable_polling.description: Enables polling for updates for an integration configuration entry.

config_entry_id.name: Config entry
config_entry_id.description: The integration configuration entry to enable polling for.

homeassistant_delete_all_orphaned_entiti.name: Delete all orphaned entities
homeassistant_delete_all_orphaned_entiti.description:
Deletes all orphaned entities that no longer have an integration that claim/provide them. Please note, if the integration was just removed, it might need a restart for Home Assistant to realize they are orphaned.
**WARNING** Entities might have been marked orphaned because an integration is offline or not working since Home Assistant started. Calling this action will delete those entities as well.

homeassistant_list_orphaned_database_ent.name: List all orphaned database entities
homeassistant_list_orphaned_database_ent.description: Lists all orphaned database entities unclaimed by any integration.

safe_mode.name: Safe mode
safe_mode.description: If the restart should be done in safe mode. This will disable all custom integrations and frontend modules.

force.name: Force restart
force.description: Force the restart. WARNING! This will not gracefully shutdown Home Assistant, it will skip configuration checks and ignore running database migrations. Only use this if you know what you are doing.

statistic_id.name: Statistics ID
statistic_id.description: The statistics ID (entity ID) to import for.

source.name: Source
source.description: The source of the statistics data.

unit_of_measurement.name: Unit of measurement
unit_of_measurement.description: The unit of measurement of the statistics.

has_mean.name: Has a mean
has_mean.description: If the statistics has a mean value.

has_sum.name: Has a sum
has_sum.description: If the statistics has a sum value.

stats.name: Statistics
stats.description: A list of mappings/dictionaries with statistics to import. The dictionaries must contain a "start" key with a datetime string other valid options are "mean", "sum", "min", "max", "last_reset", and "state". All of those are optional and either an integer or a float, except for "last_reset" which is a datetime string.

select_random.name: Select random option
select_random.description: Select a random option for a select entity.

options.name: Options
options.description: Limits the options to select from. If not provided, all options will be considered.

input_select_random.name: Select random option
input_select_random.description: Select a random option for an input_select entity.

options.name: Options
options.description: Limits the options to select from. If not provided, all options will be considered.

input_select_shuffle.name: Shuffle options
input_select_shuffle.description: Shuffles the list of selectable options for an `input_select` entity. This is not persistent and will be undone once reloaded or Home Assistant restarts.

input_select_sort.name: Sort options
input_select_sort.description: Sorts the list of selectable options for an `input_select` entity. This is not persistent and will be undone once reloaded or Home Assistant restarts.

number_decrement.name: Decrease value
number_decrement.description: Decrease a number entity value by a certain amount.

amount.name: Amount
amount.description: The amount to decrease the number with. If not provided, the step of the number entity will be used.

number_increment.name: Increase value
number_increment.description: Increase a number entity value by a certain amount.

amount.name: Amount
amount.description: The amount to increase the number with. If not provided, the step of the number entity will be used.

number_max.name: Set maximum value
number_max.description: Set a number entity to its maximum value.

number_min.name: Set minimum value
number_min.description: Set a number entity to its minimum value.

input_number_decrement.name: Decrease value
input_number_decrement.description: Decrease an input number entity value by a certain amount.

amount.name: Amount
amount.description: The amount to decrease the input number with. If not provided, the step of the number entity will be used.

input_number_increment.name: Increase value
input_number_increment.description: Increase an input number entity value by a certain amount.

amount.name: Amount
amount.description: The amount to increase the input number with. If not provided, the step of the number entity will be used.

input_number_max.name: Set maximum value
input_number_max.description: Set an input number entity to its maximum value.

input_number_min.name: Set minimum value
input_number_min.description: Set an input number entity to its minimum value.

input_number_create.name: Create an input number
input_number_create.description: Create a new input number helper on the fly.

name.name: Name
name.description: The name of the new input number helper.

input_number_id.name: Input number entity ID
input_number_id.description: The entity ID for the new input number helper. The input_number. prefix is added automatically. If not provided, the entity ID will be generated based on the name.

min.name: Minimum
min.description: The minimum value of the input number.

max.name: Maximum
max.description: The maximum value of the input number.

initial.name: Initial value
initial.description: The initial value of the input number when Home Assistant starts.

step.name: Step size
step.description: The step size of the input number.

mode.name: Display mode
mode.description: The display mode of the input number, which can be either a slider or an input box.

icon.name: Icon
icon.description: The icon to use for the input number helper.

unit_of_measurement.name: Unit of measurement
unit_of_measurement.description: The unit of measurement of the input number.

input_number_delete.name: Delete an input number
input_number_delete.description: Delete an input number helper. This works only with input numbers created and managed via the UI. Input numbers created and managed in YAML cannot be managed by Spook.

person_add_device_tracker.name: Add a device tracker
person_add_device_tracker.description: Add a device tracker to a person.

entity_id.name: Person
entity_id.description: The person entity ID to add the device tracker to.

device_tracker.name: Device tracker
device_tracker.description: The device tracker entity ID to add to the person.

person_remove_device_tracker.name: Remove a device tracker
person_remove_device_tracker.description: Remove a device tracker from a person.

entity_id.name: Person
entity_id.description: The person entity ID to remove the device tracker from.

device_tracker.name: Device tracker
device_tracker.description: The device tracker entity ID to remove from the person.

repairs_create.name: Create issue
repairs_create.description: Manually create and raise an issue in Home Assistant repairs.

description.name: Description
description.description: The description of the issue. Supports Markdown.

issue_id.name: Issue ID
issue_id.description: The issue can have an identifier, which allows you to cancel it later with that ID if needed. It also prevents duplicate issues to be created. If not provided, a random ID will be generated.

domain.name: Domain
domain.description: This field can be used to set the domain of the issue. For example, by default (if not set), it will use "spook". This causes Spook to be shown in the logo/image of the issue. If you set it to "homeassistant", the Home Assistant logo will be used, or use "hue", "zwave_js", "mqtt", etc. to use the logo of that integration.

severity.name: Severity
severity.description: The severity of the issue. This will be used to determine the priority of the issue. If not set, "warning" will be used.

persistent.name: Persistent
persistent.description: If the issue should be persistent, which means it will survive restarts of Home Assistant. By default, issues are not persistent.

repairs_ignore_all.name: Ignore all issues
repairs_ignore_all.description: Ignore all issues currently raised in Home Assistant Repairs.

repairs_remove.name: Remove issue
repairs_remove.description: Removes a manually created Home Assistant repairs issue. This action can only remove issues created with the `repairs_create` action.

repairs_unignore_all.name: Unignore all issues
repairs_unignore_all.description: Unignore all issues currently raised in Home Assistant Repairs.

timer_set_duration.name: Set duration
timer_set_duration.description: Set duration for an existing timer.

duration.name: Duration
duration.description: New duration for the timer, as a timedelta string.

zone_create.name: Create a zone
zone_create.description: Create a new zone in Home Assistant on the fly.

icon.name: Icon
icon.description: Icon to use for the zone

latitude.name: Latitude
latitude.description: Latitude of the zone. This can be a template.

longitude.name: Longitude
longitude.description: Longitude of the zone. This can be a template.

zone_update.name: Update a zone
zone_update.description: Update properties of a zone on the fly.

entity_id.name: Entity ID
entity_id.description: The ID of the entity (or entities) to update.

icon.name: Icon
icon.description: Icon to use for the zone

latitude.name: Latitude
latitude.description: Latitude of the zone. This can be a template.

longitude.name: Longitude
longitude.description: Longitude of the zone. This can be a template.

zone_delete.name: Delete a zone
zone_delete.description: Delete a zone. This works only with zones created and managed via the UI. Zones created and managed in YAML cannot be managed by Spook.

entity_id.name: Entity ID
entity_id.description: The ID of the entity (or entities) to remove.
